zotero.abstractNote: Simulation-based inference (SBI) has become a powerful tool for performing Bayesian inference in scientific models where the likelihood is intractable but the simulator is available. However, SBI often requires a large number of simulations, which can be prohibitively expensive for complex models. Multifidelity methods address this by leveraging a large number of cheap, low-fidelity simulations alongside a small number of expensive, high-fidelity simulations. In this work, we introduce a novel multifidelity SBI framework that learns to map low-fidelity simulations to high-fidelity ones, allowing for efficient posterior estimation. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmarks and a complex climate model, showing that it can achieve significant speedups while maintaining high accuracy.
